chapter: JEFP109 – The Book That Saved the Earth
title: The Book That Saved the Earth – Medium VSAQ

questions.question: Why is the twentieth century called the ‘Era of the Book’?
questions.answer: Because books were the main source of knowledge and learning.

questions.question: What is Think-Tank’s opinion about Earth?
questions.answer: He considers Earth a primitive and insignificant planet.

questions.question: Why does Think-Tank consult the mirror?
questions.answer: To admire himself and confirm his superiority.

questions.question: Why do the Martians enter a library?
questions.answer: They mistake it for an Earth structure without understanding its purpose.

questions.question: Why do the Martians think books are sandwiches?
questions.answer: Because they lack knowledge of Earth’s culture and habits.

questions.question: What role does Captain Omega play?
questions.answer: She leads the Martian mission on Earth.

questions.question: Why do the Martians listen to books?
questions.answer: They believe books are communication devices.

questions.question: Why do nursery rhymes confuse the Martians?
questions.answer: They take figurative language literally.

questions.question: What does Think-Tank think Earthlings can grow?
questions.answer: He thinks they can grow metals like silver.

questions.question: Why does Think-Tank fear the rhyme ‘Hey Diddle Diddle’?
questions.answer: He believes animals on Earth have advanced abilities.

questions.question: Why does Think-Tank panic after the Humpty Dumpty rhyme?
questions.answer: He thinks the rhyme refers to his own downfall.

questions.question: What decision does Think-Tank take about Mars?
questions.answer: He orders the evacuation of Mars.

questions.question: Why is the invasion of Earth cancelled?
questions.answer: The Martians feel threatened by Earth’s knowledge.

questions.question: Who replaces Think-Tank as the ruler of Mars?
questions.answer: Noodle replaces Think-Tank.

questions.question: Why do Earthlings resume contact with Mars?
questions.answer: To establish peaceful relations.

questions.question: What lesson do Martians learn from Earth?
questions.answer: They learn the importance of reading.

questions.question: What makes the play humorous?
questions.answer: The Martians’ misunderstanding of simple things.

questions.question: What does the library symbolise?
questions.answer: It symbolises knowledge.

questions.question: Why is Think-Tank called ‘Great and Mighty’?
questions.answer: The title is used ironically.

questions.question: What role does fear play in the play?
questions.answer: Fear leads to wrong decisions.

questions.question: What genre does the play belong to?
questions.answer: It belongs to science fiction comedy.

questions.question: What quality of Noodle makes him a good leader?
questions.answer: His intelligence and ability to learn.

questions.question: What is the central theme of the play?
questions.answer: Knowledge is more powerful than weapons.

questions.question: Why is the title of the play appropriate?
questions.answer: Because a book saves Earth from invasion.

questions.question: What message does the play convey?
questions.answer: Ignorance leads to fear and failure.
